proposed_from.topic: Matched minimax EXCESS-LENGTH rate for distribution-free (conformal) coverage of a stochastic-intervention (modified-treatment-policy / incremental) potential outcome Y^g in [0,1] under a heavy density-ratio tail, regime gamma0 in (1,2). Weighted-conformal weight w=g/pi (density ratio, E[w|X]=1), overlap = the density-ratio tail P(w>u)<=C u^{-gamma0} so E[w^2]=inf. Over class W(gamma0,s,beta) [GPS sup-rate n^{-s/(2s+d)}, outcome regression beta-Holder], the minimax high-probability excess length over the oracle weighted-conformal interval of connected, training-conditionally-(1-alpha)-valid distribution-free intervals is ≍ n^{-rho}, with rho(gamma0,s,beta)=(gamma0-1)*min{a,1/gamma0}, a=s/(2s+d)+beta/(2beta+d): (UPPER) a tail-aware cross-fit DOUBLY-ROBUST-ORTHOGONAL clipped-WCP (clip w at 1/b_n, b_n=n^{-min{a,1/gamma0}}, Neyman-orthogonal conformity score) is training-conditionally valid with high-probability excess length <= n^{-rho}; (LOWER, the core) a Le Cam two-point construction differing only in the thin-overlap heavy-w region forces excess length >= n^{-rho}. Named objects: the conformal excess-length exponent rho and its linear (gamma0-1) density-ratio-tail dependence. The finite-sample distribution-free companion to Dorn arXiv:2504.13273; differs from Yang-Kuchibhotla-Tchetgen 2024 (asymptotic/generic), Verhaeghe 2409.20412 / Schroder 2407.03094 (WCP method, imported coverage, no rate/exponent/converse), and Diaz-Hejazi/Kennedy (asymptotic efficient estimation, no distribution-free coverage).
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The proposal claims no novelty for causal change of measure, robust compatible-law coverage, or the singleton known-ratio reduction. Its distinct open frontier is the sharp population and sample length cost of a graded causal certificate induced by a finite conditionally normalized ratio list: ordinary cells have a numerical width tolerance delta, while unresolved cells are confined by a certified upper-ratio tail with gamma in (1,2). Jin, Ren, and Candes provide the robust-coverage baseline, and Kiyani, Pappas, and Hassani provide generic covariate-shift length optimization, but neither supplies this tail-local connected-interval modulus or matched high-probability regret question. Qiu, Dobriban, and Tchetgen Tchetgen establish the boundary case of completely unknown shift; the certified finite list and envelope are the additional information whose informative finite-sample cost is characterized here.
seed_list: A matched heavy-tail oracle-excess-length frontier for learned stochastic-intervention WCP, with a shell-local Le Cam converse.
A critical-tail (gamma=1) logarithmic correction and an honest adaptation-impossibility theorem for clipped causal WCP.
A uniform shell-envelope theorem that makes clipped-WCP length comparisons class-uniform rather than law-specific.
Sharp sensitivity bounds for causal conformal prediction when the stochastic-intervention density ratio is only approximately correct.
A tail-index-adaptive causal WCP oracle inequality, or a matching adaptation penalty, over gamma in (1,2).
The smallest coverage-target inflation required by an estimated stochastic-intervention ratio, together with a lower bound.
A known-ratio heavy-tail benchmark: minimax excess length of exact WCP relative to a specified score oracle.
Finite-sample robust WCP under a certified density-ratio uncertainty set, with the certificate's heavy-tail length price.

seed_details.one_line: A matched heavy-tail oracle-excess-length frontier for learned stochastic-intervention WCP, with a shell-local Le Cam converse.

anchor_paper.author: Schröder et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-sample causal conformal intervals for continuous treatments with estimated propensity scores.

seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from weighted exchangeability plus Dorn's clipping analysis.
seed_details.rebuttal: Does not land on its face: neither source derives an interval-length converse; it fails instead because estimated weights do not preserve the claimed exact conditional distribution-free validity.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Fails well-posedness: the interval class, oracle score, and coverage mode are not fixed.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: A critical-tail (gamma=1) logarithmic correction and an honest adaptation-impossibility theorem for clipped causal WCP.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn
anchor_paper.claim: Thresholded doubly robust t-statistics can be asymptotically calibrated under weak overlap.

seed_details.referee_objection: A routine transcription of a scalar-estimation threshold calculation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The quantile/interval target and honest adaptive calibration are absent from Dorn, but exact learned-weight coverage must first be relaxed or certified.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Retained only after replacing exact learned-weight validity by a high-probability coverage-error criterion.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: A uniform shell-envelope theorem that makes clipped-WCP length comparisons class-uniform rather than law-specific.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn
anchor_paper.claim: Weak-overlap thresholding has explicit tail and nuisance bookkeeping.

seed_details.referee_objection: Just adds uniform constants to an existing clipping proof.
seed_details.rebuttal: The archived proposal shows that this comparison is precisely the missing nontrivial step, but it cannot repair learned-weight exact validity.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Requires two-sided shell regularity; an upper tail bound alone cannot supply uniform oracle comparability.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: Sharp sensitivity bounds for causal conformal prediction when the stochastic-intervention density ratio is only approximately correct.

anchor_paper.author: Yang et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Doubly robust asymptotic calibration and latent-shift sensitivity analysis.

seed_details.referee_objection: Robust validation already addresses shifted target laws.
seed_details.rebuttal: Cauchois et al. is not causal-density-ratio partial identification and Yang et al. does not give sharp finite-sample endpoints or a heavy-tail length frontier.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Has a named sensitivity radius and computable endpoint representation rather than a qualitative robustness claim.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: A tail-index-adaptive causal WCP oracle inequality, or a matching adaptation penalty, over gamma in (1,2).

anchor_paper.author: Wang and Goel
anchor_paper.claim: Clipped learned-ratio WCP obtains corrected dataset-conditional coverage.

seed_details.referee_objection: Data-driven inflation already makes clipping adaptive.
seed_details.rebuttal: That result gives a correction, not a sharp tail-index-adaptive causal length exponent or its lower bound.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Must state adaptation relative to the corrected-coverage benchmark, not exact weighted exchangeability.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: The smallest coverage-target inflation required by an estimated stochastic-intervention ratio, together with a lower bound.

anchor_paper.author: Tibshirani et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Exact weighted conformal coverage holds with a known likelihood ratio.

seed_details.referee_objection: Qiu et al. already prove learned-shift finite-sample prediction sets are uninformative.
seed_details.rebuttal: A structured, certified causal ratio class may quantify the escape from that impossibility, but not under the present unrestricted GPS-rate premise.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Requires a verifiable ratio certificate or explicitly asymptotic coverage; otherwise the impossibility lands.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: A known-ratio heavy-tail benchmark: minimax excess length of exact WCP relative to a specified score oracle.

anchor_paper.author: Tibshirani et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Weighted exchangeability yields exact coverage under a known likelihood ratio.

seed_details.referee_objection: Known weights make the problem a standard weighted empirical-quantile calculation.
seed_details.rebuttal: A nontrivial lower bound may remain for a fixed oracle score, but it does not deliver the advertised learned-GPS exponent.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Retained as a benchmark, not as a substitute for the stated topic.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

seed_details.one_line: Finite-sample robust WCP under a certified density-ratio uncertainty set, with the certificate's heavy-tail length price.

anchor_paper.author: Cauchois et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Robust predictive validation over divergence neighborhoods.

seed_details.referee_objection: An f-divergence robustification is just imported robust validation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The causal ratio certificate, connected interval endpoint calculation, and heavy-tail price are not supplied there.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The uncertainty set must be observable/certified; an estimated point ratio alone is not a finite-sample certificate.
seed_details.burned_reason: The field-tier matched excess-length frontier is unreachable under this frozen kernel; field novelty requires a new criterion, stronger quantile assumptions, list-learning/identification structure, or new comparison theory.

proposed_from.literature_map: Tibshirani et al. (2019) proves exact WCP from known likelihood ratios, not learned ones. Qiu et al. (2023) proves finite-sample coverage under unknown covariate shift is uninformative. Yang et al. (2024) gives asymptotic doubly robust calibration and records a finite-sample impossibility boundary. Schröder et al. (2025) gives causal continuous-treatment conformal intervals with estimated propensity but no heavy-tail length frontier. Dorn (2025) gives clipped doubly robust scalar inference, not conformal quantile length. Wang and Goel (2026) obtain corrected expected/dataset-conditional coverage under learned unbounded ratios, not exact weighted exchangeability. Kennedy et al. (2024) provides the localized fuzzy-hypothesis lower-bound technique, but it cannot overcome an invalid coverage premise. The archived stat_ate_overlap_decay program confirms that upper-tail assumptions alone do not yield the required class-uniform shell comparison.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: technical_internal_limitation / ass:certificate-containment
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The finite ratio list, its induced envelope, and containment of the actual ratio are externally certified rather than estimated from the calibration sample.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All coverage and both frontier questions are conditional on a fixed externally certified finite list containing w.

banked_reason: D0.5: TERMINAL-BELOW-FIELD — faithfulness and mathematics pass, but the fixed-list sample degeneracy and existential SR_infinity population obstruction do not meet the requested field novelty floor.
banked_proposal_promise_gap: The proposed matched n^{-rho} learned-weight frontier was not supportable; the maximized sound result is a below-field fixed-list robust-WCP degeneracy and support-rich obstruction diagnostic.
